question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction with an extremely large number of key-value pairs in the Metadata field of MempoolTransactionRequest, causing the JSON parser to allocate a large map and consume heap memory before the mempoolAPIService discards the request, enabling memory-based DoS that prevents gossip transaction lookups?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /mempool endpoint is publicly exposed in the Kubernetes ingress (path: /rosetta/mempool) without authentication, allowing the attacker to send unlimited probe requests to confirm mempool gossip is disabled and map the attack surface without any credential requirement?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on POST /mempool/transaction to send crafted requests at the rate limit boundary (average: 10/host), staying just below the rate limit while still consuming enough server resources to degrade gossip transaction query performance for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool requests with a valid NetworkRequest but with the SubNetworkIdentifier set to an unexpected value, causing the rosetta-sdk-go asserter to reject the request and return an error, and by doing so at scale, generate enough 4xx/5xx responses to trigger the Traefik circuit breaker and block gossip endpoint access?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction with a TransactionIdentifier containing special characters or SQL injection payloads in the Hash field, and given that the mempoolAPIService discards the request without sanitization, determine whether the asserter or upstream logging of the hash value introduces any secondary injection vulnerability that could affect gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the mempoolAPIService.Mempool() always returns (nil, ErrNotImplemented) to use the /mempool endpoint as a reliable 'always-error' oracle, sending requests at high volume to deliberately inflate the NetworkErrorRatio above 0.25 and trigger the Traefik circuit breaker, causing a complete denial of service for all Rosetta API endpoints including gossip-related ones?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mempoolAPIService.MempoolTransaction() always returns (nil, ErrNotImplemented) to use the /mempool/transaction endpoint as a reliable error generator, flooding it to push ResponseCodeRatio(500, 600, 0, 600) above 0.25 and trigger the Traefik circuit breaker, denying all gossip transaction queries across the entire Rosetta API?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool requests with a Transfer-Encoding: chunked body that is never completed (incomplete chunked body), causing the HTTP server to hold the connection open waiting for the final chunk, consuming connection slots and preventing legitimate gossip mempool queries from being served?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction with a Content-Length header that is larger than the actual body, causing the HTTP server to wait for additional bytes that never arrive, holding the connection open and consuming server resources that would otherwise serve legitimate gossip transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the mempoolAPIService is registered only in the online router (newBlockchainOnlineRouter) and not the offline router, to infer the server's online/offline mode by probing /mempool and comparing response codes, gaining intelligence about the node's operational state that could be used to time gossip-prevention attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction with a NetworkIdentifier that has a valid blockchain but an invalid network name, causing the asserter to return a specific error code that leaks information about the valid network names supported by the node, which could be used to craft more targeted gossip-prevention attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool with a request body containing a JSON array instead of a JSON object, causing the rosetta-sdk-go controller to return a parsing error, and by flooding with such malformed requests, exhaust the error handling capacity of the server and prevent legitimate gossip mempool queries from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction with a completely empty JSON body ({}), causing the asserter to reject the request due to missing required fields, and by flooding with such requests at the rate limit boundary, consume enough server resources to degrade gossip transaction query performance?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request deduplication in mempoolAPIService to send identical POST /mempool requests in rapid succession, causing the server to process each request independently without any caching or deduplication, wasting resources and degrading gossip endpoint availability?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request deduplication in mempoolAPIService to send identical POST /mempool/transaction requests with the same TransactionIdentifier.Hash in rapid succession, causing the server to process each request independently and wasting resources that could serve legitimate gossip transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user use multiple source IPs (e.g., via a botnet or cloud instances) to bypass the Traefik inFlightReq limit of 5 per IP and the rateLimit of 10 per host, sending 5 concurrent requests from each of N IPs to generate N*5 concurrent requests against the mempoolAPIService, overwhelming the server and preventing gossip endpoint availability?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user use multiple source IPs to bypass per-IP rate limits and flood POST /mempool/transaction, generating enough 501 (Not Implemented) responses to push the NetworkErrorRatio above 0.25 and trigger the Traefik circuit breaker, causing a complete denial of service for gossip transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ErrNotImplemented error returned by Mempool() is a fixed, pre-defined error object (not dynamically generated), to confirm through response fingerprinting that the gossip mempool is permanently disabled and will never return transaction data, enabling the attacker to make informed decisions about transaction submission timing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ErrNotImplemented is returned regardless of the TransactionIdentifier value in MempoolTransaction(), meaning there is no way to distinguish between 'transaction not in mempool' and 'mempool not implemented', to submit duplicate transactions knowing that no gossip-level deduplication check is exposed, potentially causing network-level issues?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool requests with a very large number of concurrent connections (connection flood), given that the mempoolAPIService has no connection-level throttling beyond what Traefik provides, exhausting the Go HTTP server's goroutine pool and preventing legitimate gossip mempool queries from being handled?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction requests with a TransactionIdentifier.Hash containing only whitespace characters, which may pass JSON parsing but fail asserter validation, generating 500 errors at high volume to trigger the circuit breaker and deny gossip transaction queries to all users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the mempoolAPIService context parameter is discarded (_ context.Context) to send requests with already-cancelled contexts, causing the server to process the request fully (including asserter validation and error return) even though the client has already disconnected, wasting server resources and degrading gossip endpoint capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the discarded context (_ context.Context) in MempoolTransaction() to send requests with very short deadlines, causing the HTTP framework to time out the response but the service to continue processing, creating goroutine leaks that accumulate over time and eventually exhaust server memory, preventing gossip transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool with a NetworkRequest.Metadata field containing a JSON object with thousands of keys, causing the JSON unmarshaller to allocate a large map before the mempoolAPIService discards the request, enabling a memory exhaustion attack that prevents gossip mempool queries from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send POST /mempool/transaction with a MempoolTransactionRequest containing a NetworkIdentifier with a very long Network string (e.g., 1MB), causing the asserter to allocate memory for the string before rejecting it, enabling a memory exhaustion attack against gossip transaction endpoints?
